growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: corkrey.
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

sec.method: measured production rate for GEM validation (simulated 4.490)

growth_records.cite: Song Y et al. Functional cooperation of the glycine synthase-reductase and Wood-Ljungdahl pathways for autotrophic growth of Clostridium drakei. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2020). DOI:10.1073/pnas.1912289117. [PMC7132306]
growth_records.method: reported (experimental mu; model-simulated mu was 0.178 h-1)
growth_records.snippet: The experimental and simulated growth rates were 0.184 and 0.178 h-1 for the fructose condition ... The respective experimental and simulated acetate production rates were 4.524 and 4.490 mmol.gDW-1.h-1 ... for the fructose ... condition
growth_records.notes: Heterotrophic fructose condition. Experimental growth_rate 0.184 h-1 (in-text; simulated 0.046 err, simulated fructose value 0.178). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.184 = 3.77 h (calculated). Acetate secretion 4.524 mmol/gDW/h is a MEASURED (experimental) value used to validate GEM iSL771 (simulated 4.490). Medium = DSM 135 (pH 7.0), full composition given only in SI Appendix, so medium_composition_stated=false; only fructose 5 g/L stated in main text. Biological duplicates. No experimental fructose uptake rate reported in main text.

sec.method: measured production rate for GEM validation (simulated 3.291)

growth_records.cite: Song Y et al. Functional cooperation of the glycine synthase-reductase and Wood-Ljungdahl pathways for autotrophic growth of Clostridium drakei. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2020). DOI:10.1073/pnas.1912289117. [PMC7132306]
growth_records.method: reported (experimental mu; model-simulated mu was 0.046 h-1)
growth_records.snippet: the growth rates were ... 0.044 and 0.046 h-1 for the ... H2/CO2 condition ... acetate production rates were ... 3.278 and 3.291 mmol.gDW-1.h-1 for the ... H2/CO2 conditions
growth_records.notes: Autotrophic H2/CO2 (80:20, 200 kPa) condition. Experimental growth_rate 0.044 h-1 (in-text; simulated 0.046). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.044 = 15.75 h (calculated). Acetate secretion 3.278 mmol/gDW/h is MEASURED (experimental) for GEM iSL771 validation (simulated 3.291). No experimental H2 or CO2 uptake rate reported for C. drakei in main text (only model MCMC flux distributions, e.g. glycine-synthase flux 2.09, GSRP acetyl-P flux 1.98, RGP glyA flux 0.10 - these are in-silico Markov-chain-Monte-Carlo sampled fluxes, NOT experimental, so omitted from mfa_fluxes). 13C tracing was qualitative label-fraction (47.3% serine labeled on fructose), not quantitative flux MFA. Medium DSM 135, full composition in SI Appendix only.
